name: Russian Jewish Cabbage Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Core the cabbage and place it in a large saucepan. Cover with water and bring to a boil until softened.
2. Drain off the water and set aside.
3. Chop the onion, carrots, and celery stalks.
4. In a small bowl, dampen bread with water. Add hamburger meat, egg, salt, and pepper; mix well.
5. Remove thick stem base from cabbage leaves and place them in the saucepan.
6. Spread some of the meat mixture on each leaf, fold, and roll, placing the seam side down.
7. Arrange chopped onions, carrots, and celery on top of the cabbage rolls.
8. Pour tomato juice over all and caramelized sugar (1 cup) in a heavy skillet until reduced.
9. Add the remaining ingredients, including juice from 2 lemons, to the cabbage roll mixture.
10. Simmer uncovered for 2 hours.
This recipe uses a large saucepan, but a 13-inch cake pan can also be used. The temperature is not specified, so 350°F (175°C) is recommended. After cooking, the carrots and celery can be laid horizontally as decoration to create an attractive dish that is well worth the effort.
